items.prompt: I need to compare total sales across different regions to understand which markets are driving the most revenue. Please sort the regions from highest to lowest sales so the ranking is immediately clear. This should help inform where we focus our expansion efforts.

Can you show how revenue changes month over month throughout the year? I want to identify any seasonal trends or recurring patterns in monthly revenue. This will help us plan promotions and inventory more effectively.

I’d like to understand the relationship between ad spend and revenue across campaigns. Plotting these two metrics should help reveal whether higher spend is actually driving better returns. This will guide how we allocate budget across channels.

Please show the distribution of customer ratings across all orders to assess overall satisfaction. I want to see how frequently different rating levels occur and whether most customers are satisfied. This will help identify if there are issues in specific rating ranges.

Compare the average profit across product categories to see which segments are most profitable. This should help us identify where margins are strongest and where improvements are needed. Use the category dimension to break down the profit metric clearly.

Show how sales have trended over time by month for each region so we can compare growth patterns. I want to see whether certain regions are accelerating faster than others. This will help prioritize regional sales strategies.

Analyze delivery time across different shipping methods to understand consistency and delays. I’m particularly interested in the spread and any outliers in delivery time by shipping method. This will help evaluate which options provide the most reliable service.

Create a view of sales by month and region to identify seasonal peaks across different geographies. I want to quickly spot which regions perform best during specific months. This insight will help with regional planning and demand forecasting.

Show the top 10 countries ranked by total revenue so we can identify our most important markets. Make it easy to compare how much each country contributes to overall revenue. This will support decisions on international growth priorities.

Plot conversion rate against traffic volume and break it down by customer segment to understand performance differences. I want to see whether higher traffic actually translates into better conversion across segments. This will help refine targeting and acquisition strategies.

I want to track how daily average temperature changes over time to identify seasonal patterns and unusual spikes. Please plot temperature by date so we can see trends and fluctuations clearly. Highlight any periods where temperature deviates significantly from the norm.

I need to understand how order values are distributed across all transactions. Plot the distribution of order value so we can see where most purchases fall and whether there are high-value outliers. This will help us better understand customer purchasing behavior.

Compare profit margins across product categories and customer segments to identify where we are most profitable. Use a grouped view so differences between segments within each category are easy to interpret. I want to see which combinations drive the strongest margins.

Analyze how monthly ad spend relates to revenue over time to evaluate marketing efficiency. Plot both metrics by month so we can compare trends and identify periods where spending did or did not translate into revenue growth. This should help assess ROI patterns.

Examine the relationship between product price and units sold to understand demand sensitivity. Plot price against units sold across products to see if higher prices are reducing sales volume. This will help inform pricing strategy decisions.

Assess how sales vary across different regions to identify consistency and outliers. Show the distribution of sales by region so we can compare variability and detect regions with unusually high or low performance. This will help prioritize regional strategies.

I want to evaluate how revenue, profit, and ad spend are related to each other. Show the correlations between these metrics to identify strong positive or negative relationships. This will help us understand how marketing investment impacts financial outcomes.

Track how monthly order volume changes over time for each customer type. Break out the trends by customer type so we can compare behavior patterns between segments. The goal is to identify which segments are growing or declining over time.

Compare average delivery time across regions to identify where delays are most severe. Sort the regions so the slowest delivery times are easy to spot. This will help prioritize operational improvements in underperforming areas.

Analyze how revenue is distributed across different customer segments. Show the distribution of revenue with a clear distinction between segments so we can identify which groups contribute most and whether variability differs. This will inform targeting and retention strategies.

Analyze how inventory levels relate to sales across different products to evaluate stock efficiency. I want to see whether higher inventory is actually driving higher sales or leading to overstock in certain product categories.

Track daily website traffic over time and break it down by traffic source to understand channel performance. The goal is to identify which channels are driving the most consistent traffic and where fluctuations occur.

Compare total sales across regions and product categories to understand contribution patterns. I want to see how each category performs within each region and which combinations drive the most revenue.
Examine the distribution of delivery times across different shipping types to assess service reliability. The goal is to identify which shipping methods have the most variability and longest delays.
Analyze order volume by weekday and hour to identify peak activity periods. This should help determine when customer demand is highest and inform staffing or promotion timing decisions.
Track monthly revenue over time to understand overall growth trends. I want to clearly see how revenue changes from month to month and identify any periods of acceleration or decline.
Identify the top-performing products based on total sales to understand which items drive the most revenue. This will help prioritize inventory and marketing efforts toward high-performing products.

Explore the relationship between profit and revenue across products while accounting for order volume. The goal is to identify which products generate strong profits relative to their revenue and scale.
Evaluate the distribution of monthly revenue growth rates to understand business volatility over time. I want to see how often we experience strong growth versus declines across different months.

Compare sales trends over time across different product categories to identify patterns and seasonality. I need to see each category separately to understand which ones are growing, declining, or behaving differently over time.

I want to understand how our customer base is distributed across different segments. Please show the total customer count by segment so we can identify which groups are driving the most engagement. This will help prioritize targeting and retention strategies.

We need to evaluate whether weather conditions are influencing sales performance. Plot sales against temperature across different dates to identify any correlation between the two variables. This should help us understand if warmer or colder days impact revenue trends.

I’d like to analyze how profit varies across different regions. Show the distribution of profit by region so we can identify variability and any extreme outliers. This will help us spot underperforming or high-risk markets.

We need a clear view of how conversion rate differs across device types and regions. Visualize conversion rate by device type and region to highlight any patterns or performance gaps. This will help optimize marketing and user experience strategies.

Track how daily order volume has changed over time to identify trends or seasonality. Plot the number of orders by date with a smooth trend to make patterns easier to interpret. This will help with demand forecasting and operational planning.

I want to break down total revenue by customer segment to understand contribution differences. Show how each segment contributes to overall revenue so we can compare their relative importance. This will support decisions around segment-focused strategies.

We need to understand the age distribution of our customer base. Visualize customer ages to see how they are spread across different age groups. This will help inform demographic targeting and product positioning.

Assess how efficiently our marketing spend is converting into results. Plot ad spend against conversion rate across campaigns or channels to identify any relationship. Include a trend to help determine whether higher spend is leading to better performance.

I need to compare how profit trends evolve over time across different regions. Show monthly profit by region so we can evaluate regional performance patterns side by side. This will help identify which regions are improving or declining over time.

Identify the regions generating the highest order volumes. Show the top 5 regions ranked by total orders so we can quickly see where demand is strongest. This will help guide resource allocation and regional strategy decisions.

I want to understand how shipping cost varies across different carriers. Plot the distribution of shipping costs segmented by carrier to identify which providers tend to be more expensive or inconsistent. This will help us evaluate cost efficiency and negotiate better rates.

Analyze the relationship between revenue and profit across different product categories. Create a scatter plot to see whether higher revenue consistently translates into higher profit by category. This will help identify underperforming categories with poor margins.

Track how inventory levels change over time to spot potential stockouts or overstock situations. Use a time series view of inventory by date to highlight trends and seasonality. This will support better supply chain and replenishment planning.

Provide a clear view of how sales performance varies across regions and product categories. Use a heatmap to highlight high and low sales combinations by region and category. The goal is to quickly identify strong markets and underperforming segments.

Compare the distribution of order values across different customer types. Use this to understand whether certain segments, like new vs returning customers, tend to generate higher-value orders. This insight will help guide targeting and retention strategies.

Examine how revenue trends evolve month over month across different years. Plot monthly revenue with clear markers and distinguish each year to compare seasonal patterns. This will help identify growth trends and recurring peaks or dips.

Compare total profit across countries to identify which markets are underperforming. Display profit by country in ascending order so it's easy to spot the weakest contributors. This will help prioritize regions that need attention or cost optimization.

Evaluate delivery performance by analyzing delivery time across different regions. Plot the distribution of delivery times segmented by region to identify delays or inconsistencies. This will help pinpoint areas where logistics improvements are needed.

Explore how website traffic relates to conversions and revenue generation. Create a scatter plot of traffic versus conversions, using revenue to indicate the size of each point. This will help identify high-value segments and optimize marketing performance.

Break down sales performance by product category across different regions. Use faceted bar charts to compare how each category performs within each region side by side. The goal is to identify regional preferences and category-level growth opportunities.

Analyze monthly revenue and profit over the past year to understand how growth is impacting margins. Plot both metrics by month using dual y-axes so we can compare their trends clearly. Highlight any periods where revenue increased but profit lagged to identify margin pressure.

Build a dashboard view comparing total sales across regions alongside the overall sales trend over time. Use bars to show regional sales and a line to show total sales by month in a separate subplot. This should help identify which regions are driving overall performance changes.

Track revenue by month to identify any significant declines that may need investigation. Plot the time series and annotate points where revenue drops sharply compared to the previous period. The goal is to quickly pinpoint when and how often these declines occur.

Compare total sales across product categories and customer segments to understand contribution mix. Create a stacked bar chart showing sales by category with segments layered within each bar. Ensure the legend clearly distinguishes segments so we can assess which groups drive category performance.

Evaluate the relationship between ad spend and revenue across marketing channels or campaigns. Plot a scatter chart with ad spend on one axis and revenue on the other, and include a regression line to highlight the trend. This will help determine how effectively spend is translating into revenue.

Examine the distribution of order values to understand purchasing behavior across customers. Create a histogram of order value and adjust bin sizes to reveal meaningful patterns. Annotate peaks to highlight the most common order ranges.

Analyze how promotional periods impact sales trends over time by month. Plot sales as a line chart and visually highlight the date ranges where promotions were active. This should make it easy to see whether promotions correspond with noticeable increases in sales.

Compare revenue and profit trends over time to assess overall business performance. Create side-by-side subplots showing monthly revenue in one chart and monthly profit in another. This layout should make it easier to spot differences in growth patterns between the two metrics.

Assess delivery time performance across regions or shipping methods to identify inconsistencies. Use box plots to show the distribution of delivery time for each group and annotate notable outliers. This will help highlight areas with unusually slow deliveries.

Understand how key metrics like sales, profit, and ad spend relate to each other across the dataset. Create a correlation heatmap with labeled values to show the strength of relationships between these metrics. This should help identify which factors are most closely associated with performance.

Show how monthly sales have trended across different regions over the past year using a multi-line view. I want to quickly identify which region is consistently outperforming others. Highlight the top-performing region so it stands out clearly for leadership review.

Compare average profit across product categories to understand which areas of the business are most profitable. Include variability using error bars so we can assess consistency within each category. This should help identify categories with unstable or risky profit performance.

Analyze the relationship between customer spend and order frequency across different customer segments. Use distinct marker symbols for each segment so we can easily compare their behavior. The goal is to identify which segments drive higher revenue and how their purchasing patterns differ.

Visualize the monthly revenue trend over the past year to understand overall business performance. Mark and annotate the highest and lowest revenue points so we can investigate what drove those extremes. This will help highlight key periods of growth or decline.

Show how website traffic from different channels has evolved over time on a monthly basis. Use a stacked area view to understand each channel’s contribution to total traffic. This should help us see which channels are driving growth and whether any are declining.

Examine the distribution of order values across all transactions to understand customer spending behavior. Include a cumulative distribution line to show what proportion of orders falls below certain value thresholds. This will help us identify pricing opportunities and typical purchase ranges.

Compare how conversion rate and website traffic have changed over time on a monthly basis. Use a dual-axis view so we can see whether increases in traffic are actually leading to better conversion. The goal is to identify periods where traffic quality may have dropped or improved.

Build a dashboard-style view showing trends for revenue, profit, and number of orders over time by month. Each metric should be in its own subplot so we can compare patterns side by side. This will help us understand whether growth in orders is translating into higher profit and revenue.

Compare total sales across regions for each year to understand geographic performance trends. Use grouped bars so we can easily see how each region performs year over year. The goal is to identify regions with consistent growth or decline.

Analyze the relationship between revenue and cost across different products to identify efficient performers. Highlight the cluster of points where revenue is high but cost is low so we can spot top-performing products. This will help guide decisions on where to focus investment or expansion.

Analyze how revenue has trended over time by month and assess the stability of those trends. Include a shaded confidence interval around the revenue line so we can understand uncertainty and volatility. Highlight any periods where variability is especially high to inform forecasting risk.

Examine the distribution of sales across different product categories to identify which categories have the most variability. Use a box plot to compare median sales, spread, and outliers by category. Make the layout clear enough to quickly spot categories with inconsistent performance.

Identify the top-performing countries based on total sales and compare their contributions. Display a bar chart of the top countries with annotations showing exact sales values for quick reference. This should help stakeholders easily see which markets are driving the most revenue.

Evaluate how order volume varies by weekday and hour to uncover peak demand periods. Use a heatmap to show the density of orders across these time dimensions with a clear color scale. This will help operations teams optimize staffing and delivery planning.

Compare revenue trends over time across multiple product categories to see which ones are growing or declining. Plot each category as a separate line so patterns and seasonality are easy to distinguish. The goal is to identify which categories are driving overall performance changes.

Investigate the relationship between revenue and profit across different regions to understand efficiency. Create a scatter plot with error bars to reflect variability or uncertainty in both metrics. This should highlight regions where high revenue does not translate into strong profitability.

Break down total sales by product category and compare their percentage contributions across regions. Use a stacked bar chart to show how each category contributes to overall sales in each region. This will help identify dominant categories and gaps in the product mix.

Track revenue trends over time by month and identify how major business events impacted performance. Add vertical lines to mark key events like promotions or product launches. This will help determine whether those events drove meaningful changes in revenue.

Analyze the distribution of order values to understand customer purchasing behavior. Create a histogram of order amounts and overlay a normal distribution curve to assess how closely the data follows a typical pattern. This will help identify skewness and potential outliers in spending.

Compare the distribution of customer lifetime value across different customer segments. Use subplots of histograms so each segment can be evaluated side by side. This will help determine which segments drive the most value and how consistent their spending patterns are.

Track monthly revenue over time and compare it against cumulative revenue to understand long-term growth trends. Use a dual-axis view so we can see both period performance and overall accumulation clearly. Highlight any months where revenue growth significantly accelerates or slows down.

Compare total sales across product categories to identify which categories are driving the most revenue. Include detailed hover information such as profit, number of orders, and average order value for each category. The goal is to give stakeholders a richer understanding of category performance without cluttering the chart.

Analyze the relationship between advertising spend and revenue across different marketing channels. Highlight and annotate the top 5 data points with the highest revenue to understand what drives peak performance. This should help identify which channels deliver the best return at higher spend levels.

Evaluate sales performance across product categories and months to identify seasonal trends and weak spots. Use a heatmap with annotated values so it’s easy to compare performance at a glance, and reverse the color scale to emphasize lower sales areas. The goal is to quickly spot underperforming periods that need attention.

Build a multi-panel dashboard to monitor sales, profit, and conversion rate across regions over time. Each panel should focus on a different metric but align on the same time dimension for easy comparison. This should help leadership quickly assess overall performance and identify regions that are underperforming across multiple metrics.

Show how revenue trends evolve over time by month, using a filled area chart to emphasize growth patterns. Break this down by product category so we can see which categories contribute most to total revenue. The goal is to highlight both overall momentum and category-level contributions.

Rank regions by total sales to identify the top-performing markets. Sort the bars in descending order and visually highlight the leading region to make it stand out. This should help quickly communicate which regions are driving the majority of revenue.

Compare the distribution of delivery time across different regions to understand variability in service performance. Use separate subplots for each region so differences in spread, median, and outliers are easy to spot. The goal is to identify regions with inconsistent or unusually slow delivery times.

Examine the relationship between product price and number of orders across different categories. Use a scatter plot where color intensity represents total revenue to highlight high-value combinations. This will help identify which products balance strong demand with high revenue contribution.

Visualize how cumulative sales build over time by product category to understand contribution to overall growth. Use a stacked line chart to show how each category adds to total sales across months. The goal is to identify which categories consistently drive growth and which lag behind.

Compare average delivery time across regions and product categories to understand where performance is inconsistent. Use grouped bars by category within each region and include error bars to show variability in delivery time. Add annotations to highlight the region-category combination with the worst average delivery time.

Analyze how daily revenue has changed over time across different months and identify any major spikes or declines. Include interactive zooming so stakeholders can focus on specific date ranges like holiday periods or campaign windows. The goal is to quickly explore short-term vs long-term revenue trends.

Examine the distribution of order values across different regions to see how customer spending behavior varies. Overlay multiple histograms for each region so we can compare spread, skewness, and typical order size. This should help identify regions with unusually high or low purchasing patterns.

Investigate the relationship between revenue and profit across different product categories. Include a scatter plot with marginal histograms to show the distribution of both metrics and highlight any correlation patterns. This will help identify categories that generate high revenue but low profit.

Compare monthly orders and revenue trends to understand how sales volume translates into earnings over time. Use bars for orders and a line on a secondary axis for revenue, segmented by month. The goal is to identify periods where order growth does not align with revenue performance.

Evaluate sales performance across product categories and regions to identify clusters of similar behavior. Display a heatmap of revenue with hierarchical-style ordering to group similar regions and categories together. This should reveal patterns such as consistently strong or weak combinations.

Compare monthly revenue trends across multiple years to identify seasonality and year-over-year changes. Use subplots for each year so patterns can be easily compared side by side. The goal is to determine whether certain months consistently drive higher revenue.

Analyze total sales by region while also breaking down contributions by product category within each region. Use a combination of grouped and stacked bars to show both overall comparisons and internal composition. This will help identify which categories drive regional performance differences.

Explore the relationship between profit and revenue across products to identify outliers and unusual performance. Highlight and annotate products that have high revenue but low or negative profit. This analysis should help flag potential pricing or cost issues.

Assess how revenue and profit have evolved over time to understand margin trends. Use a dual-axis chart with both metrics plotted by month to compare their movement. The goal is to identify periods where profit diverges from revenue growth.
